extra.message: User input from request.form['expression'] is passed directly to eval(), allowing arbitrary Python code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

extra.message: User input from request.form['address'] is concatenated directly into a shell command string passed to rp() (intended as popen, imported at line 4). This allows arbitrary OS command execution.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: XML parser is configured with no_network=False and dtd_validation=True, allowing external entity resolution. User-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] is parsed with this insecure configuration, enabling XXE attacks including local file disclosure and SSRF.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from request.form['name'] is embedded into a template string using % formatting, then passed to render_template_string(). This enables Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI), allowing remote code execution via Jinja2 template syntax injection.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Flask secret key 'Harry Potter And The Deathly Hallows' is hardcoded in source code. This allows attackers to forge session cookies, bypass authentication, and execute other session-based attacks if the source code is disclosed.

The result of eval() on user-supplied expression is embedded directly into HTML without HTML-escaping, allowing reflected Cross-Site Scripting via crafted expression return values containing JavaScript payloads.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The output of the OS command executed via user-supplied address is embedded directly into HTML without HTML-escaping, allowing reflected Cross-Site Scripting via command output containing JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Parsed XML output (sourced from untrusted user input in request.form['xml']) is embedded directly into HTML without HTML-escaping, allowing reflected Cross-Site Scripting if the parsed XML contains JavaScript payloads.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Flask debug mode is enabled (app.debug = True). In production, this exposes the Werkzeug debugger which allows arbitrary Python code execution through the interactive debugger console and leaks sensitive information through detailed error pages.

The 404 error handler passes the raw error object to the template (error.html line 20 renders {{ error }}). This can disclose internal server paths, stack traces, and other sensitive information to users.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information
